text: vv. 6-13: Mk. 14.3; Jn. 12.1. According to St John, this incident took place six days before the paschal feast.

We know from St John (e.g. 18.28) that the ‘first day of unleavened bread’, that is the paschal feast on the 14th day of the month Nisan, was kept by the Jews on the Friday that year. Possibly the Galileans had a different date, since it seems that the month was dated from the first night when the paschal moon wasobserved, and one cloudy night might falsify the reckoning. But the Greek Fathers understand St Matthew here as calling Thursday ‘the day before the days of unleavened bread’. This would avoid any appearance of discrepancy. On such a view, it seems that our Lord deliberately anticipated the paschal meal by twenty-four hours; or we may suppose that the meal described in verses 20 and following was not the meal mentioned in verse 19, but that of the night before it.
‘Hereafter’; some think this was said in irony, meaning ‘from now on’, but the sense may be ‘sleep at some other time, not now’.
